policies.title: 1.1.1 Extensions must have a single purpose
policies.fix: Ensure your extension has a single, narrow function. Bundling unrelated features will be rejected.

policies.title: 1.1.2 Describe your extension accurately
policies.fix: Provide a clear, accurate description (12-132 chars). Do not impersonate other companies or extensions.

policies.title: 1.1.3 Functionality - no broken URLs or blocked links
policies.fix: Verify every URL/link the extension uses works and is not blocked.

policies.title: 1.1.4 Search and discovery - <=7 relevant search terms
policies.fix: In Partner Center, list at most 7 unique search terms relevant to your extension.

policies.title: 1.1.5 Provide appropriate listing details
policies.fix: Make the listing distinctive and informative about functionality.

policies.title: 1.1.6 Stability and performance
policies.fix: Test for crashes, freezes, and excessive resource use before submission.

policies.title: 1.1.7 No obfuscated code
policies.fix: Ship readable, reviewable source. Minification is allowed; obfuscation that hides intent is not.

policies.title: 1.1.8 No silent altering of browser settings
policies.fix: Disclose any change to homepage / search provider / new tab and require explicit user consent.

policies.title: 1.2.1 Content Security Policy
policies.fix: Remove unsafe-eval and remote script-src URLs from content_security_policy.

policies.title: 1.2.2 No malware, spyware, or phishing
policies.fix: Confirm no malicious behavior. Account suspension is permanent if malware is detected.

policies.title: 1.2.3 Disclose dependencies on other software
policies.fix: Clearly disclose any external dependency required for primary functionality in the description.

policies.title: 1.2.4 Updates only via Partner Center
policies.fix: Do not self-update outside of the Edge Add-ons store.

policies.title: 1.3.1 Provide test user credentials if login is required
policies.fix: Add demo credentials in Submission Options > Notes for certification or explain why none can be provided.

policies.title: 1.3.2 Server services must be available during review
policies.fix: Keep any backend the extension relies on online during certification.

policies.title: 1.4.1 Compatibility across platforms
policies.fix: Detect unsupported devices at launch and show a clear requirements message.

policies.title: 1.4.2 Responsive user experience
policies.fix: Ensure prompt startup, responsiveness, and graceful shutdown.

policies.title: 1.5.1 Collect personal information only when necessary
policies.fix: Collect only what is required for a disclosed user-facing feature, with consent.

policies.title: 1.5.2 Maintain a privacy policy
policies.fix: Publish and link a privacy policy specific to your extension and Microsoft Edge.

policies.title: 1.5.3 Sharing data with third parties requires opt-in
policies.fix: Obtain in-product opt-in consent before sharing user data; never engage in data brokering.

policies.title: 1.5.4 Sharing information of non-users
policies.fix: Obtain written consent and offer withdrawal for any non-user PII you publish.

policies.title: 1.5.5 Transmit information securely
policies.fix: Use HTTPS for all data transport. Remove plaintext http:// endpoints.

policies.title: 1.5.6 Highly sensitive information requires consent
policies.fix: Obtain explicit consent before collecting health, financial, or biometric data.

policies.title: 1.6 Request only necessary permissions
policies.fix: Drop any permission not actively used. No 'future-proof' permissions allowed.

policies.title: 1.7 Localize claimed languages
policies.fix: Localize description and UI in every language the extension claims to support.

policies.title: 1.8.1 Paid features - use third-party purchase API
policies.fix: Use a secure third-party payment API for goods/services. Never convert in-product currency to real money.

policies.title: 1.8.2 Disclose paid features and price ranges
policies.fix: Clearly state in-product purchase types, price ranges, and trial scope/terms.

policies.title: 1.9.1 Notifications follow developer policies
policies.fix: All notification content is subject to the same policies as extension content.

policies.title: 1.9.2 Notifications must identify their source
policies.fix: Do not obscure the originating extension in any notification.

policies.title: 1.9.3 Notifications must not contain confidential data
policies.fix: Strip sensitive content from notifications.

policies.title: 1.9.4 Notifications must relate to the extension
policies.fix: Do not push unrelated promotional messages.

policies.title: 1.10.1 Advertising is not the primary purpose
policies.fix: Advertising must not be the main content or required to use the extension.

policies.title: 1.10.2 Ad content follows Microsoft Advertising policies
policies.fix: Comply with Microsoft Advertising policies and the Microsoft Store App Developer Agreement.

policies.title: 1.10.3 Quality of advertising
policies.fix: Do not interfere with displayed ad visibility/quality; do not exist primarily to drive ad clicks.

policies.title: 1.10.4 Promotion ad campaigns comply with Creative specs
policies.fix: Ad creatives and landing pages must comply with Microsoft Advertising creative specs.

policies.title: 1.10.5 Notify users of opt-out for interest-based ads
policies.fix: Disclose interest-based ad data sharing and an opt-out mechanism in your privacy statement.

policies.title: 1.10.6 Children-directed extensions: COPPA compliance
policies.fix: If directed at <13 year olds, declare in Partner Center and ensure ad content is age appropriate.

policies.title: 2.1 Content requirements (no objectionable content)
policies.fix: Remove discriminatory, harmful, deceptive, or malware content.

policies.title: 2.2 Names, logos, and licensed third-party content
policies.fix: Use only original or properly licensed content; respect Microsoft trademarks.

policies.title: 2.3.1 No content facilitating extreme/gratuitous harm
policies.fix: Remove content glorifying violence, weapons, or human-rights violations.

policies.title: 2.3.2 Responsibility - no risk of harm
policies.fix: Provide all required safety warnings and never disable platform safety features.

policies.title: 2.4 No defamation, libel, or threats
policies.fix: Remove defamatory or threatening content.

policies.title: 2.5 No offensive or discriminatory content
policies.fix: Remove content that promotes discrimination, hate, or violence.

policies.title: 2.6 No glamorization of alcohol, tobacco, or drugs
policies.fix: Remove content glamorizing irresponsible substance use.

policies.title: 2.7 No adult / sexually explicit content
policies.fix: Remove pornographic or sexually explicit content and references.

policies.title: 2.8 Prohibited content, services, and activity
policies.fix: No gambling facilitation, paywall circumvention, pirated content, or cryptocurrency mining.

policies.title: 2.9 No facilitation of illegal activity
policies.fix: Remove anything that encourages real-world illegal activity.

policies.title: 2.10 No excessive profanity or obscene content
policies.fix: Remove gratuitous profanity or obscene material.

policies.title: 2.11 Country/region-specific content requirements
policies.fix: Ensure content is acceptable in every targeted country/region.

policies.title: 2.12.1 Mature content rating accuracy
policies.fix: Mark Mature content honestly in Partner Center.

policies.title: 2.12.2 Ratings change - gate higher-rated content
policies.fix: Gate content above the assigned rating behind a content filter or signed-in account.

policies.title: 2.13 Promotional videos follow content policies
policies.fix: Ensure any submitted promotional video complies with all content policies.

policies.title: Reported rejection: short description must be substantive (Edge Add-ons)
policies.fix: Expand the description to clearly state what the extension does and the value to the user. One-word descriptions are routinely rejected.

policies.title: Reported rejection: extension fetches and executes remote JavaScript
policies.fix: Bundle all executable code in the package. Edge certification rejects extensions that download/eval JS at runtime.

policies.title: Reported rejection: requests <all_urls> without justification
policies.fix: Restrict host permissions to the specific origins your features require, or switch to activeTab + user gesture.

policies.title: Reported rejection: heavily minified bundles without readable sources
policies.fix: Provide unminified source or a build script in the developer notes so reviewers can audit the code.

policies.title: Reported rejection: low-quality / generic / placeholder icon
policies.fix: Provide a distinct, high-resolution icon. Stock or default icons are commonly cited as a rejection reason.
